title: US Special Forces Intercept Chinese Military Cargo Bound for IRGC
detail: American special forces raid a merchant vessel in the Indian Ocean carrying Chinese-manufactured military cargo intended for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. The seized equipment reportedly includes components for centrifuge mixers and advanced electronics for missile guidance systems that Iran has been seeking to replace after the June strikes destroyed its primary enrichment infrastructure. The interception underscores the intensified enforcement regime targeting Iran's weapons procurement networks.

media.caption: US special forces intercept merchant vessel carrying Chinese military cargo bound for Iran's IRGC

title: Iran Pursuing Parallel Nuclear Reconstruction and Diplomacy
detail: Intelligence assessments indicate Iran is pursuing a dual-track strategy: maintaining a semblance of willingness for diplomacy with the West while prioritizing the rehabilitation of air defense capabilities, restoration of its ballistic missile program, and acceleration of underground nuclear facility fortification. Satellite imagery shows new construction activity at Fordow, Iran's most hardened enrichment site, which survived the June strikes largely intact due to its deep mountain location.

sources.name: ISIS (Institute for Science and International Security)

media.caption: Satellite imagery shows new construction activity at Fordow as Iran pursues parallel nuclear reconstruction

title: Reports of $1 Billion IRGC Transfer to Hezbollah Fuel Domestic Anger
detail: Reports emerge that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps transferred more than $1 billion to Hezbollah in Lebanon during the first ten months of 2025, even as Iran's domestic economy spiraled into crisis. The revelation, circulated on social media and picked up by reformist outlets, intensifies public anger at the regime's spending priorities. Critics argue the funds could have been used to stabilize the rial or subsidize food imports at a time when household purchasing power has collapsed.

media.caption: Reports reveal IRGC smuggled over $1 billion to Hezbollah in 2025 despite economic crisis at home
